2605.20087.title: ThoughtTrace: Understanding User Thoughts in Real-World LLM Interactions
2605.20087.paper_summary: Conversational AI has now reached billions of users, yet existing datasets capture only what people say, not what they think. We introduce ThoughtTrace, the first large-scale dataset that pairs real-world multi-turn human--AI conversations with users' self-reported thoughts: their reasons for sending prompts and reactions to assistant responses. ThoughtTrace comprises 1,058 users, 2,155 conversations, 17,058 turns, and 10,174 thought annotations collected across 20 language models. Our analysis shows that ThoughtTrace captures long-horizon, topically diverse interactions, and that thoughts are semantically distinct from messages, difficult for frontier LLMs to infer from context, diverse in content, and tied to conversation stages. We further demonstrate the utility of thoughts for downstream modeling. First, thoughts improve user-behavior prediction as inference-time context. Second, thought-guided rewrites provide fine-grained alignment signals for training personalized assistants. Together, ThoughtTrace establishes user thoughts as a new data modality for studying the cognitive dynamics behind human--AI interaction and provides a foundation for building assistants that better understand and adapt to users' latent goals, preferences, and needs.
2605.20087.paper_summary_zh: 對話式人工智慧現在已經達到數十億用戶，但現有數據集僅捕捉人們所說的內容，而非他們的想法。我們介紹了ThoughtTrace，這是第一個大規模數據集，將現實世界的多輪人類--人工智慧對話與用戶自我報告的想法配對：他們發送提示的原因和對助手回應的反應。ThoughtTrace包含1,058名用戶、2,155個對話、17,058個回合和10,174個思考註釋，這些數據是從20個語言模型中收集的。我們的分析顯示，ThoughtTrace捕捉了長期、多主題的互動，並且思考在語義上與信息是不同的，對於前沿的LLM來說，從上下文中推斷這些思考是困難的，內容多樣，並且與對話階段相關。我們進一步展示了思考在下游建模中的實用性。首先，思考作為推斷時的上下文可以改善用戶行為預測。其次，思考引導的重寫為訓練個性化助手提供了細緻的對齊信號。總之，ThoughtTrace確立了用戶思考作為一種新的數據模式，用於研究人類--人工智慧互動背後的認知動態，並為構建能更好理解和適應用戶潛在目標、偏好和需求的助手提供了基礎。
2605.20087.author: Chuanyang Jin et.al.
2605.20087.authors: Chuanyang Jin, Binze Li, Haopeng Xie, Cathy Mengying Fang, Tianjian Li, Shayne Longpre, Hongxiang Gu, Maximillian Chen, Tianmin Shu
